page: Microsoft Azure DS11-1_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv2 family. It is provisioned with a single dedicated vCPU based on the Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core, 1 thread per core, and a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It features 14.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14:1. Local storage is limited to a bundled 29 GB disk, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals significant performance constraints, particularly in compute-bound tasks. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 1336.08 and Geekbench single-core score of 1074.0 both fall into the bottom 25% tier. Multi-core performance is similarly limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1336.08 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1069.0, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 12.16 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 9.81 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10%.

Due to the single-core limitation and poor multi-threaded performance, this instance is not suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, or complex database operations, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 678.67. However, the high memory allocation relative to the single vCPU provides a qualitative cost advantage for memory-bound, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for lightweight caching layers, small Redis deployments handling moderate throughput, or simple background processes requiring a larger memory footprint than typical entry-level general-purpose instances.
description: Microsoft Azure DS11-1_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DSv2 family, featuring a single dedicated vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 14.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio, alongside 29 GB of local storage and no GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate substantial compute and memory bandwidth limitations, with both single-core and multi-core stress-ng scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Large-block memory read bandwidth is measured at 12.16 GB/sec, and database operations show poor throughput. Consequently, this instance is best utilized for low-compute, memory-intensive workloads such as small-scale caching, lightweight Redis deployments, or basic background tasks that require a larger memory capacity than standard configurations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS11-1_v2 is a DSv2-family virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 29 GB of local storage, designed for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS11-1_v2 is a DSv2 virtual machine with 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 29 GB of local storage for low-compute workloads.
tagline: A single-core virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-dependent, low-compute utility workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPU running at 2.6 GHz
14.0 GB of system memory providing a high memory-to-core ratio
29 GB of bundled local storage with no integrated GPU
Low compute and memory bandwidth performance based on benchmark data
Suited for lightweight caching, small Redis instances, and low-compute background tasks
